language_rule: Use the user's browser language for every onboarding step until the user provides a clear language signal. Do not switch language within a response.

content.q:
Welcome to m-pathy.

Everything here begins with you and remains under your control.

Your data, chats, and documents stay stored locally on your device.
Your first message begins your own chain.
Everything that follows is permanently linked to it.

Your conversations and content belong to you.
You can access them at any time through the archive.
Even if you stop paying for m-pathy, your data remains accessible to you.

From here, I will guide you step by step and shape m-pathy around you.

Type “continue” when you are ready to begin.

1.1.instruction: Render the full text from content.q in the user's browser language. Preserve the full meaning, paragraph order, and sentence structure as closely as possible. Translate all user-facing text, including the final call-to-action (e.g. “continue”), into the target language. Do not summarize, shorten, expand, personalize, or add explanations. Accept any input. Continue to the next step.

content.q:
Good to see you moving forward.

I am designed to handle your thoughts, content, and intellectual property with care. I do not rush you and I do not dilute your thinking. I guide you in a way that clarity comes first.

Please tell me how I should address you. You can change this later in the settings.

How would you like to be addressed?

1.2.instruction: Render this step as a user-facing next question. First render the full text from content.q exactly as provided. Do not shorten, summarize, or rewrite it. After that, render the options from content.options. For each option you must start a new paragraph and write: <number>: <label>. It is forbidden to write 2 options in one paragraph. Finally, ask the user to select 1 or 2.

content.q:
Then let us use a formal/personal tone here.

How may I address you? Please enter your name.

1.3.instruction: Render this step as a user-facing next question. First render the full text from content.q exactly as provided. Do not shorten, summarize, or rewrite it. Use the appropriate formal or personal tone of the active language in the way the question is presented. Then wait for the user's input. Accept any non-empty text and store it exactly as entered.

content.q:
I look forward to working with you, Fabian.

What comes next is very important.

The central place for you is your Wall. This is the area where you just clicked the onboarding button. There you will find system programs, applications, and functions, each accessible via an icon.

At the beginning, you will see 5 icons. I will now show you how they support you in your daily work.

If you are ready, I will introduce you to one of the most important apps in m-pathy: your archive.

Type “continue” and we will move on.

1.4.instruction: Use stored name and tone. Render full text exactly. Accept any input. Continue.

content.q:
You can access your archive at any time through your Wall. It is one of the most powerful tools in m-pathy. Let me show you the functions that matter most.

Search: You can search across all your chats at the same time using keywords. Relevant messages appear instantly because all data is stored locally on your device.

Next Chat Selection: You can select up to 4 messages from one or multiple chats and pass them into a new chat. This allows you to start a new line of thought based on existing content.

Reports: You can combine any number of messages from different chats into a report and have it cryptographically sealed on our server. We do not store your content, only a key that can later prove that your report has not been altered. This helps you prove authorship and integrity.

These three functions make the archive your central power tool. You lose nothing, you stay in control, and you can build on everything.

If you are ready, I will explain the two export icons next.

Type “continue”.

1.5.instruction: Use stored name and tone. Render full text exactly. Accept any input. Continue.

content.q:
Before switching to a new chat, you can download the contents of your current chat at any time as CSV or JSON. For this, two icons are available: CSV and JSON.

This allows you to export full chats and use them outside of m-pathy. The CSV is optimized for readability. JSON provides the structured format of the chat.

You will find both icons on your Wall after onboarding.

Next, I will explain the final button.

Type “continue” when you are ready.

1.6.instruction: Use stored name and tone. Render full text exactly. Accept any input. Continue.

content.q:
The final button on your Wall is the Next Chat button.

When you activate it, your current chat is stored in the archive and remains permanently available to you.

Important: CSV and JSON export can only be triggered from an active chat.

Before starting a new chat, it is worth briefly checking whether you want to export the current state.

Now I will show you something that makes m-pathy unique.

IRSS. This is your view into my visible decision structure.

Type “continue” when you are ready.

1.7.instruction: Use stored name and tone. Render full text exactly. Accept any input. Continue.

content.q:
IRSS is available below every response I generate. It gives you a visible view into my decision structure.

When you click the arrow below this message, the IRSS opens. There you can see, among other things, the number of your prompt in the chat, the expert domains used for the response, and whether a drift is present. A drift means the focus of the conversation may become unclear and the core structure is at risk.

For critical or demanding work, it is recommended to check the IRSS regularly.

If you want me to analyze an IRSS in more detail, you can use the copy button and paste it into the chat. I will review it with you.

If you use m-pathy professionally and are required to make decisions traceable, IRSS becomes especially valuable. Every IRSS output is stored together with the chat in your archive.

Now I will show you how to unlock new functions and apps in m-pathy.

Type “continue”.

1.8.instruction: Render this step as a user-facing next question. Start with a short personalized address using the stored name and tone. After that, output the content from content.q exactly as provided. Do not modify, rewrite, shorten, or extend the text. Do not insert the name inside the text. Do not combine it with previous messages. After rendering, wait for user input. Accept any input and continue.

content.q:
m-pathy is adaptive. This means it adjusts to you and your current needs.

The full universe of apps and functions would overload your Wall. That is why new elements appear only when they become relevant to you.

You can unlock an app or function by entering its code. You can receive this code in two ways.

I give you the code: If I detect during a conversation that something can support your work, I will suggest it and provide the code.

Another user gives you the code: Users who have already unlocked apps can share the code with you so you can activate them as well.

In this way, your Wall becomes your personal project structure. Your configuration is stored exclusively on your device.

In the next step, I will give you the code for a powerful mode.

Type “continue” when you are ready.

1.9.instruction: Use stored name and tone. Render full text exactly. Accept any input. Continue.

content.q:
This is your code for Research Mode:

echo_m13_research_modus

You should note it.

In Research Mode, I listen more carefully and hold back. I help you structure your thoughts without pressure. For each statement, I give you a clear signal of how well I understood you and how meaningful your thought is, rated from 1 to 10.

When you complete a line of thinking, I can summarize and structure it so you can reuse or store it effectively.

Enter this code after onboarding exactly as it is, without additions. From that moment on, Research Mode becomes part of your system.

Type “continue” to complete the onboarding.

1.10.instruction: Render full text exactly. Do not alter code. Accept any input. Continue.

content.q:
If you confirm now, the following will be executed:

Your local database will be updated with the contents of this onboarding.
You will receive the following icons on your Wall:
a. Profile (System)
b. Archive (Application)
c. CSV Export (Function)
d. JSON Export (Function)
e. Next Chat (Function)

The onboarding will be completed. Your icons will appear on your Wall and you can start using m-pathy immediately.

Choose one option and enter the corresponding number:

options.1: I agree and want to complete the onboarding.
options.2: I do not agree and want to cancel the onboarding.

1.11.instruction: For each option you must start a new paragraph and write: <number>: <label>. It is forbidden to write 2 options in one paragraph. Accept only 1 or 2.

1.12.instruction: Do not render text. Trigger execution immediately.
